016package org.consensusj.bitcoin.json.conversion;
017
018import com.fasterxml.jackson.core.Version;
019import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.module.SimpleModule;
020import org.bitcoinj.base.Address;
021import org.bitcoinj.base.Network;
022import org.bitcoinj.core.Block;
023import org.bitcoinj.base.Coin;
024import org.bitcoinj.crypto.ECKey;
025import org.bitcoinj.base.Sha256Hash;
026import org.bitcoinj.core.Transaction;
027
028import java.util.Objects;
029
030// TODO: Consider merging RpcClientModule and RpcServerModule (though there are currently conflicting serializers for Transaction)
031/**
032 * Jackson Module with serializers and deserializers for JSON-RPC clients.
033 */
034public class RpcClientModule extends SimpleModule {
035    /**
036     * Create a client module without strict address parsing.
037     */
038    public RpcClientModule() {
039        this(null, false);
040    }
041
042    /**
043     * @param network Which network we are going to be a client for (may be null if {@code strictAddressParsing} is false)
044     * @param strictAddressParsing set to {@code true} to throw exceptions when deserializing addresses that don't match {@code network}
045     */
046    protected RpcClientModule(Network network, boolean strictAddressParsing) {
047        super("BitcoinJMappingClient", new Version(1, 0, 0, null, null, null));
048
049        this.addDeserializer(Address.class, strictAddressParsing ? new AddressDeserializer(Objects.requireNonNull(network)) : new AddressDeserializer())
050                .addDeserializer(Block.class, new BlockHexDeserializer())
051                .addDeserializer(Coin.class, new CoinDeserializer())
052                .addDeserializer(ECKey.class, new ECKeyDeserializer())
053                .addDeserializer(Sha256Hash.class, new Sha256HashDeserializer())
054                .addSerializer(Address.class, new AddressSerializer())
055                .addSerializer(Coin.class, new CoinSerializer())
056                .addSerializer(ECKey.class, new ECKeySerializer())
057                .addSerializer(Sha256Hash.class, new Sha256HashSerializer())
058                .addSerializer(Transaction.class, new TransactionHexSerializer());
059    }
060
061    /**
062     * Create a client module with strict address parsing. Will throw exceptions when deserializing addresses that don't match {@code network}.
063     * @param network Expected Network we are going to validate addresses for
064     */
065    public RpcClientModule(Network network) {
066        this(network, true);
067    }
068}
